label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question. The question is asking what we can infer about the Puya chilensis in England based on the passage. The passage says that the Royal Horticultural Society has been feeding the plant a diet of liquid fertilizer. The plant was planted 15 years ago, but this is the first time it has bloomed. The passage mentions that in its natural habitat, it's believed to trap animals with spikes and use their bodies as fertilizer, but in England, they're feeding it liquid fertilizer instead. Looking at the answer choices: A) feeds on man-made liquid fertilizer - This matches with the part where they mention feeding it liquid fertilizer. B) often kills sheep and other animals - The passage says this is in its natural habitat and is what is "believed," not that it's happening in England. C) has once bloomed 15 years before - The passage says it was planted 15 years ago, but this is the first time it has bloomed, so
